checks.evidence: Docs overview page states 'Attio is a revolutionary CRM platform'. Pricing page positions it as a CRM. Category 'CRM (Customer Relationship Management)' is accurate.
Auth docs describe three methods: OAuth 2.0 flow, API key generation, and HTTP Basic Authentication (username=token, password blank). All use Bearer token in Authorization header.
Pricing page shows Free plan at $0 with 'API and webhook access' and 'MCP server' checkmarks. 'Start for free, no credit card required.' No paid plan required. Self-serve signup available.

REST API docs page states 'Attio provides a public REST API which exchanges JSON over HTTPS.' Docs cover authentication, rate limits, filtering/sorting, pagination, webhooks, OAuth, attribute types, extensive endpoint reference (Core, Standard objects, SCIM 2.0, OAuth 2.0), and 11+ webhook event types.

Docs overview page lists 'MCP' as one of four parts of the Attio Developer Platform: 'Manage Attio via AI. Connect Claude, ChatGPT...'. Pricing page shows 'MCP server' checkmark on Free plan. Dedicated MCP navigation section exists in docs.

All criteria met: public REST API with comprehensive docs, OAuth 2.0 + API key + Basic auth, free self-serve tier with API/webhook/MCP access, official MCP server, and App SDK. No material blockers observed.
